info.title: Veengu Platform Integration API
info.description:
# General Informantion

Veengu integration API is available for B2B integration of Partner systems with Veengu Platform.

## Base URL

Base URL of intergration API of specific Veengu Platform's instance is provided to Partner by Platform's tenant.

## API security

All API requests to Veengu Platform must be made over HTTPS. Calls made over plain HTTP will fail. API requests without authentication will also fail.

Veengu Platform uses tenant keys and access tokens to authenticate & authorize partner's system client requests and securely grant or decline access to it's API resources.

### Tenant key/code

As a part of Partner onboarding, Veengu Platform's tenant provides partner with it's unique tenant string identifier — the tenant's code. A partner's system should always send this tenant's code in `V-Tenant` header in every API request to the Platform.

This security header is referenced in this specification as `TenantKey`.

Example:

  ```
  GET /integration/api/files/fed83815-1308-4f15-8c86-15b5ff299779
  V-Tenant: abc_tenant
  ...
  ```

### API key | Access Token

As a part of Partner onboarding, Veengu Platform's tenant provides partner with a secret key for the partner for live and test modes. A partner' system should always send test or live keys in `V-Access-Token` header in every API request to the Platform.

Example:

  ```
  POST /integration/api/files
  V-Tenant: abc_tenant
  V-Access-Token: <PROVIDED-API-KEY>
  Content-Type: application/json

  <...>
  ```

This security header is referenced in this specification as `AccessToken`.

**NOTE** Partner's API key carries privileges for creating financial transactions so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share Partner's API key in publicly accessible areas such as GitHub, client-side code and so forth.

### Profile key/ID

In some cases partner is capable to make actions on behalf of some individual and business/merchant profiles registered in the system. For example, initiate transactions or get list of registered applications. In such cases profile identifier should be provided in `V-Profile` header in such requests to the Platform.

Example:

  ```
  POST /integration/api/files
  V-Tenant: abc_tenant
  V-Access-Token: <PROVIDED-API-KEY>
  V-Profile: c29e6fae-d600-4a13-8dcc-a75cdd23c2ef
  Content-Type: application/json

  <...>
  ```

Profile identifier could be discovered when profile is registered by partner or searched.

## Channel Identification

Each API request may specify the channel type via the `V-Api-Channel` header. This header is optional: if not provided, the system will automatically apply the default value of `MIDDLEWARE`. 
This identifier helps route requests appropriately and ensures proper handling based on the source platform. 
The channel type is stored in transaction details and may affect notification delivery.

Channel code  |  Description
-----------|--------------
`MOBILE` | Mobile applications
`USSD` | USSD channel
`WEB` | Web applications
`WHATSAPP` | WhatsApp integration
`MIDDLEWARE` | External systems
`OTHER` | Other channels

## Data format

### Date and time formats

All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone.

To get the transaction created date in customer local time you use `createdAt` attribute in the transaction response and transaction history.

During transaction initiation, you can define transaction local timezone on the application client site with sending `localUTCOffset` attribute as an offset of user mobile device with the locale time zone in ISO 8601 as an offset from UTC formats:

  * ±hh:mm
  * ±hhmm
  * ±hh

### Money format

All money in API is defined as an amount object with mandatory fields `value` and `currency`.

Currency should be present as currency code in ISO 4217 alpha-3 format.

The value field should be presented as the decimal number of monetary units specified in an appropriate currency. Field pattern applies based on currency ISO 4217 definition.

## Versioning

When we make backward-incompatible changes to the API we release new dated versions. The current version is `3.1.0`. Read our API changelog to learn more about backward compatibility.

To request a specific API version for a given call, send the `V-Version` header with the requested semver value:

  ```
  GET /integration/api/files/fed83815-1308-4f15-8c86-15b5ff299779
  V-Tenant: abc_tenant
  V-Access-Token: <PROVIDED-API-KEY>
  V-Version: 3.1.0
  ...
  ```

Requests with no `V-Version` header retain the legacy (pre-`3.0.0`) behaviour. Each endpoint whose contract differs between versions documents the difference in its own description.

As a precaution, use API versioning to test a new API version before committing to an upgrade.

## Errors handling

For error handling, Veengu as a service-provider uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request. In general, error codes in the `2xx` range indicate success. Codes in the `4xx` range indicate errors with clinet's requests. Codes in the `5xx` range indicate errors with Veengu servers (these are rare).

In the case of any request processing error Veengu Platform returns an error response with appropriate HTTP status and payload of the following format:

```
{
  "type": "...",
  "code": "...",
  "message": "...",
  "details": {}
}
```

where

- `type` is the error category, one of `ACCESS_ERROR` (authentication or authorization error), `INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR` (request is malformed), `FINANCIAL_TRANSACTION_ERROR` (failed due to financial reasons) and `OTHER_ERROR` (any other error);
- `code` is the error code; supported codes are given in error schema definition for each request;
- `message` is the human-readable message indicating the actual reason of failure;
- `details` is the optional dictionary-like object containing any extra data related to the error.

Human-readable messages are given in English language only.

For each request the set of expected errors is given in this specification.

In addition following error responses could be returned for any request:

- **401 Unauthorized** \
  Request is not authenticated, API key or client ID/secret provided is invalid. \
  Error `type`=`ACCESS_ERROR`, `code`=`NOT_AUTHENTICATED`
- **403 Forbidden** \
  Request is authenticated but access is not granted. \
  Error `type`=`ACCESS_ERROR`, `code`=`NOT_AUTHORIZED`
- **400 Bad Request** \
  Request is unexpectedly invalid. \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`OTHER_CLIENT_ERROR`
- **500 Internal Server Error** \
  Something bad has happened at the service provider side. \
  Error `type`=`OTHER_ERROR`, `code`=`OTHER_SERVER_ERROR`
- **404 Not Found** \
  Requested API endpoint / resource is not handled by the service provider. \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`NOT_FOUND`

**NOTE** If request processing fails not reaching the Veengu Platform (at some gateway for example), error HTTP response would be returned not by Veengu Platform but intermediate service instead. Such responses would have no JSON body of the format specified above and may have any other body (usually empty) provided by intermediate service layer.

The system holds the funds in an escrow account and releases them only once the recipient fulfills predefined onboarding and KYC conditions.

tags.name: Account Transfer
tags.description: System-wide account-to-account transfer. Sender and recipient accounts are identified explicitly by id or account number — no profile context is required.

tags.name: Own Accounts Transfer
tags.description: Transfer between two accounts belonging to the same originator profile. Each side may be identified either by an internal account id or by an external source id.

tags.name: Direct Transfer
tags.description: Direct account-to-account transfer initiated by an originator profile, with explicit sender and recipient account ids. Includes a beneficiary lookup endpoint used to discover eligible recipients by phone or account number.

tags.name: Outgoing Transfer
tags.description: Outgoing transfer driven by a payment form. Calculates fees and other service-specific charges, then debits the originator and routes the funds via the configured payment service.

tags.name: Payout
tags.description: Payout to an external beneficiary driven by a payment form. Calculates fees and other service-specific charges before debiting the originator.

tags.name: Payroll
tags.description: Bulk salary / mass payout flow. A payroll batch is initiated by uploading a CSV file with beneficiary records; the system validates the file, issues a consent, and then executes the batch against the originator's sender account.

get.summary: Get image or media file by ID
get.description:
Retrieves files by id.

**Since integration API version `3.0.0` the endpoint no longer verifies that the request is made on behalf of the file's owning profile.** For clients sending `V-Version: 3.0.0` (or higher), the `V-Profile` header is not required and is ignored when retrieving the file.

Clients sending `V-Version` lower than `3.0.0`, or no `V-Version` header at all, retain the legacy behaviour: when the requested file has an owning profile, the `V-Profile` header must identify the same profile, otherwise the request is rejected.

302.description: Redirection to a temporary file download URL

post.summary: Propose email address for individual profile contact
post.description:
First step of two-step flow to add or edit individual profile EMAIL contact. The back-end generates a random OTP code and sends it to the provided email address. The OTP code must then be submitted via `POST /individuals/{id}/contacts/email/verify` to complete the contact update.
Available regardless of the profile's KYC level.

200.description: OTP code dispatched to the provided email address

400.description: Invalid request. Possible `code` values: `PARAMETER_MISSING` — `email` is missing; `PARAMETER_INVALID` — `email` is empty or has an invalid format; `JSON_INVALID` — malformed JSON body.

post.summary: Confirm email address for individual profile contact
post.description:
Second step of two-step flow to add or edit individual profile EMAIL contact. Submits the OTP code received by email. If the code is correct, the back-end replaces the profile's EMAIL contact with the proposed email address.
Available regardless of the profile's KYC level.

400.description: Invalid request. Possible `code` values: `PARAMETER_MISSING` — `challengeToken` or `otp` is missing; `PARAMETER_INVALID` — `challengeToken` or `otp` is empty; `JSON_INVALID` — malformed JSON body; `WRONG_ONE_TIME_CODE` — the OTP does not match (`message`: `Email update has failed. Wrong one-time code`); `TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` — the challenge is locked after too many wrong attempts (`message`: `Email update has failed. Wrong one-time code. Too many attempts`); `BAD_CHALLENGE` — the challenge token is invalid or expired (`message`: `Email update has failed. Bad challenge`).

get.summary: Get a list of payment groups
get.description:
Retrieves a list of payment groups. Each group contains a list of payment/payout channels defined as payment services. Groups could be organized into hierarchy of payment services menu: each group could has a parent defined.

For the case of intenational remittances countries are usually defined as payment groups: alpha-2 country codes are used as groups IDs to simplify naming and country list filtration.
Retrieves the specific group by its ID.

get.summary: Get a list of payment services
get.description:
Retrieves a list of available payment/payout channels that is defined as a list of payment services. 

Cursor-based pagination is used to fetch payment services. See `next` parameter description for mode details.

To get the list for a specific group use the filter parameter `group`.

parameters.name: V-Version
parameters.description:
API version. Controls the response schema for payment services:
- If >= 3.0.4: new payment service schema with structured `amount` (containing `mode`, `single`, `beneficiary`, `remitter`), top-level `beneficiary` configuration, and deprecated legacy fields (`amountMode`, `senderAmount`, `deal.beneficiary`) returned as null.
- If < 3.0.4 or missing: legacy payment service schema with flat `amount` map, `amountMode`, `senderAmount`, and `deal.beneficiary` fields.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Payment group ID to filter services by. If provided then only services of this group are returned.

parameters.name: next
parameters.description:
Cursor/limit-based pagination is supported to fetch payment services. For each returned page the `next` field in response is a unique key identifying the page next to the page currently returned. Use the `next` string from the response in subsequent request to point to the next page.

If the `next` parameter is not presented in request the first in alphabetical order services are returned in the response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of rows on one page to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

cursors.description:
`next` and `prev` cursors for page-based navigation across the list of payment services

If all services matching the search criteria are returned in single response then `cursors` field could be missing.

prev.description: Cursor pointing to the previous page of payment services.

next.description: Cursor pointing to the next page of payment services. This value should be used in `next` parameter value of subsequent 'get services' request for navigation to the next page.

- `AUTHORIZED` should be used if transaction is confirmed/completed in partner's or third-party system

referenceId.description:
At least one transaction reference ID of partner's or third-party system. Providing such references would help to correlate transaction records in Partner's / third-party systems and Veengu Platform in case of any investigation.

`id` field defines the reference _type_. It is selected by Partner, through it is better to agree on keys with Platform's tenant. Reference `id` should be a string formatted in UPPERCASE with underscores, preferably not longer than 32 chars, for example: `ABCSYSTEM_REF_ID`.

`value` is a transaction identifier or reference value in the Partner's or third-party system.

beneficiary/remitter amount/currency pair. It could be used to get indicative rates
if multi-currency  incoming transfer is planned.

**IMPORTANT** Either `beneficiaryAmount` & `remitterCurrency` or `beneficiaryCurrency` & `remitterAmount`
pairs should be provided.

beneficiaryAmount.description: Amount to be received by the beneficiary. Should be provided along with `remitterCurrency`.

remitterAmount.description: Amount to be sent by the remitter. Should be provided along with `beneficiaryCurrency`.

tags.description: Extra transaction tags, optional

details.description: Extra transaction details in the form of JSON object, optional

baseAmount.description: Beneficiary or remitter amount (`beneficiaryAmount or `remitterAmount`) provided in request

totalAmount.description: Total e-money amount to be charged from integrated partner for this transfer; value is always negative

beneficiaryAmount.description: Amount to be received by beneficiary.

remitterAmount.description: E-money amount to be charged from remitter.

instructedAmount.description: Amount to be collected from remitter. It could differ from `remitterAmount` if any cash-based fee should be charged from remitter by payer.

entries.description: Breakdown of `totalAmount` by different categories. Transfer's primary amount, fees, commisions, taxes and etc. are presented as entries of different categories. Multiple items of scame category could be presented in this list.

amount.description: Entry amount

details.description: Calculation details. Include currency conversion rates information.

sender.description: Currency conversion infromation for sender party

rate.description: Currency conversion rate used for calculation. Beneficiary currency acts as a base currency, remitter currency acts as quote currency. `remitterAmount` = `beneficiaryAmount` x `rate`

post.description:
Creates a pending consent for an account-to-account transfer. Sender and
recipient accounts may be identified by id or by account number — no
`V-Profile` header is required.

**NOTE:** `PENDING` balance transactions are only persisted to the
transaction history when the originating partner is the sender and/or
the recipient. If the originating partner is neither party, the consent
entries are still returned in the response but are not stored in any
transaction history.

Updates a previously created (`PENDING`) consent. Same body shape as the
create endpoint.

**NOTE:** `PENDING` balance transactions are only persisted to the
transaction history when the originating partner is the sender and/or
the recipient. If the originating partner is neither party, the consent
entries are still returned in the response but are not stored in any
transaction history.

type.description:
Error type defines the area to which the error belongs. Following values could be used:

`ACCESS_ERROR` — authentication or authorization error

`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR` — request is malformed or some provided parameters are invalid

`FINANCIAL_TRANSACTION_ERROR` — request processing has failed due to some financial reasons: not enough founds, balance limits reached and so on

`OTHER_ERROR` — other error that doesn't fit into any category above

code.description:
Error code indicating specific error reason. Following codes should be used:

**Access errors** \
`NOT_AUTHORIZED` — request is authenticated but access is not granted \
`NOT_AUTHENTICATED` — request is not authenticated

**Invalid request errors** \
`PARAMETER_MISSING` — some mandatory parameter/property is missing in request \
`PARAMETER_INVALID` — some parameter/property in request has an invalid value \
`RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS` — unable to create new resource (payment for example) as it already exists \
`RESOURCE_MISSING` — requested resource (payment for example) is missing \
`RESOURCE_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested resource exists but not available for operation

**Financial transaction errors** \
`CURRENCY_NOT_SUPPORTED` — amount currency is not supported \
`LIMIT_REACHED` — limit has been reached: min/max operation amount bounds exceeded and so on \
`NOT_SUFFICIENT_FUNDS` — operation is not allowed as source account/deposit has not enough funds \
`PARTY_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested party (beneficiary for example) is not available for operation (blocked for example)\
`OPERATION_NOT_APPLICABLE` — requested operation is not applicable for selected party \
`PAYMENT_INSTRUMENT_UNAVAILABLE` — requested payment instrument is not available for operation

**Other errors** \
`NOT_FOUND` — requested resource/endpoint is not found \
`OTHER_CLIENT_ERROR` — any other client error \
`OTHER_SERVER_ERROR` — any other server error

details.description: Optional error details in JSON form

transaction_schema.description:
On the Veengu platform, transactions are represented by the transaction object.  The attributes of a given transaction object depend on the operation type. Some attributes are required for all transaction objects, such as:
operationType – Categorizes the financial business operation. For the complete list of types, see the "Operation Types" section.
status – The current status of the transaction. This is the only field that can change after the transaction object has been created.
amount – Amount of the transaction. The float number of monetary units specified in an appropriate currency. Field pattern  ^\d{1,13}\.\d{1,3}$
Each transaction is associated with two accounts that participate in the transaction. The accounts owners can be an individual or a business.

amount.description:
Payment amount configuration. Response format depends on `V-Version` header:
- V-Version >= 3.0.4: contains structured amount guidelines with `mode` and separate guidelines for `single`, `beneficiary`, and `remitter` currencies.
- V-Version < 3.0.4 or missing: flat map of currency alpha-3 code → amount guidelines (legacy format).

mode.description:
Amount input mode.
- `FIXED` — only fixed amount could be used. Amount value is returned in `/payments/check` response.
- `SUGGESTED` — suggested amount is returned in `/payments/check` response. Any amount could be used.
- `DEFAULT` — no amount is demanded/suggested by the Platform.
- `BENEFICIARY_REMITTER` — separate amounts for beneficiary and remitter currencies.

single.description: Amount guidelines for single-currency payments. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

denomination.description: Allowed amount denomination indicating number of digits after the decimal separator.

hint.description: Optional textual human-readable hint for payment amount
hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

beneficiary.description: Amount guidelines for beneficiary currency. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

remitter.description: Amount guidelines for remitter currency. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

amountMode.description:
Payment amount mode. Use `amount.mode` instead.
- `FIXED` — only fixed amount could be used. Amount value is returned in `/payments/check` response.
- `SUGGESTED` — suggested amount is returned in `/payments/check` response. Any amount could be used.
- `DEFAULT` — no amount is demanded/suggested by the Platform.
- `BENEFICIARY_REMITTER` — separate amounts for beneficiary and remitter currencies.

anyTransaction_schema.description:
In the Veengu ecosystem transactions are represented by the transaction object. The
attributes of a given transaction object depend on the operation type. Some attributes are
required for all transaction objects, such as:

operationType – Categorizes the financial business operation. For the complete list of types,
see the "Operation Types" section.

status – The current status of the transaction. This is the only field that can change after
the transaction object has been created.

amount – Amount of the transaction. The float number of monetary units specified in an
appropriate currency. Field pattern  ^\d{1,13}\.\d{1,2}$

Each transaction is associated with two accounts that participate in the transaction. The
accounts owners can be a individual or a business.

payrollRecord_schema.description: Single record in a payroll batch — one beneficiary row from the uploaded CSV.

id.description: Internal id of the payroll record.

rowNumber.description: 1-based row number in the original CSV (the header row is row 1, so the first data record is row 2).

accountNumber.description: Beneficiary account number, as supplied in the CSV.

amount.description: Amount and currency to pay this beneficiary.

beneficiaryName.description: Beneficiary name, as supplied in the CSV.

phoneNumber.description: Beneficiary phone number, as supplied in the CSV.

processingStatus.description: Processing status of the record once the batch is being executed. Same set of values as transaction/consent statuses.

validationStatus.description: Outcome of the pre-execution validation performed on the record.

payrollCheckResponse_schema.description: Result of validating a payroll CSV file. The response carries the parsed batch contents plus an `isOk` flag indicating whether the file passed validation. When the file is valid (`isOk: true`) the returned `fileId` can be used in `POST /payrolls/consent`.

fileId.description: Identifier of the stored payroll file. Present only when the file passed validation (`isOk: true`); absent otherwise.

isOk.description: `true` when every record in the file passed validation, `false` when one or more records failed (see each record's `validationStatus` in `data.records` for details).

instructedAmount.description: Total instructed amount derived from the file.

id.description: Internal id of the sender account.

description.description: Optional human-readable description parsed from the file.

recordsTotal.description: Number of beneficiary records in the file.

payrollConsentRequest_schema.description: Request body for creating a payroll consent. The CSV file referenced by `fileId` must have been previously uploaded via `POST /payrolls/check`, which validates its contents and returns the `fileId` to use here.

fileId.description: Identifier of the previously uploaded and validated payroll CSV file. Returned by `POST /payrolls/check`.

amount.description: Expected total amount of the payroll batch. The server re-validates the CSV against this value and rejects the request on mismatch.

numberOfRecords.description: Expected number of beneficiary records in the batch. The server re-validates the CSV against this value and rejects the request on mismatch.

originatorProfileId.description: Identifier of the profile that owns the sender account. Required — the call fails when omitted.

teamMemberId.description: Optional id of a business team member acting on behalf of the originator. Only applicable when the originator profile is a business and the team member belongs to that business.

payroll_schema.description: Payroll batch object. A payroll groups a list of beneficiary records submitted together against a single sender account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payroll batch was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the payroll batch.

status.description: Current status of the payroll batch.

actor.description: Subject that initiated the payroll. For a payroll initiated by a business team member this is the corresponding employee; otherwise it is the integration identity that issued the request.

type.description: Type of the actor.

instructedAmount.description: Total amount instructed across all records.

postedAmount.description: Total amount actually posted once the batch has been executed. Absent until execution starts.

id.description: Internal id of the sender account.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the payroll batch.

recordsTotal.description: Total number of beneficiary records in the batch.

recordsProcessed.description: Number of beneficiary records already processed. Equals `recordsTotal` once the batch is fully executed.

records.description: Beneficiary records of the batch. Only returned by detail endpoints (`GET /payrolls/{id}`, `POST /payrolls/consent`, `POST /payrolls`); omitted in list responses.

cursors.description: Pagination cursors for traversing the result set.

next.description: Cursor token to fetch the next page. Pass it back as the `next` query parameter on the subsequent call. Absent when no further pages are available.

payrolls.description: Page of payroll batches. Records are omitted from each entry — use `GET /payrolls/{id}` to retrieve the full record list.

name.description: Unique tag name. Latin letters (A-Z, a-z), digits (0-9), underscores (`_`) and pluses (`+`) are allowed.

profileTypes.description: Profile types the tag may be applied to.

specialities.description: Tag specialities used by the platform for built-in roles.

shouldPromoteToTransactionTag.description: When `true`, the tag is propagated as a `PROFILE=<name>` (and party-scoped `PROFILE:PAYER=...` / `PROFILE:PAYEE=...`) tag on transactions involving a profile bearing this tag.

editableViaIntegrationApi.description: When `true`, the tag can be added to or removed from a profile via the integration API (`PUT /individuals/{id}/tags`, `PUT /businesses/{id}/tags`). When `false` or absent, attempts to add or remove this tag via the integration API are rejected with HTTP 400. Default `false`.

type.description:
Error type defines the area to which the error belongs. Following values could be used:

`ACCESS_ERROR` — authentication or authorization error

`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR` — request is malformed or some provided parameters are invalid

`FINANCIAL_TRANSACTION_ERROR` — request processing has failed due to some financial reasons: not enough founds, balance limits reached and so on

`OTHER_ERROR` — other error that doesn't fit into any category above

code.description:
Error code indicating specific error reason. Following codes should be used:

**Access errors** \
`NOT_AUTHORIZED` — request is authenticated but access is not granted \
`NOT_AUTHENTICATED` — request is not authenticated

**Invalid request errors** \
`PARAMETER_MISSING` — some mandatory parameter/property is missing in request \
`PARAMETER_INVALID` — some parameter/property in request has an invalid value \
`RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS` — unable to create new resource (payment for example) as it already exists \
`RESOURCE_MISSING` — requested resource (payment for example) is missing \
`RESOURCE_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested resource exists but not available for operation

**Financial transaction errors** \
`CURRENCY_NOT_SUPPORTED` — amount currency is not supported \
`LIMIT_REACHED` — limit has been reached: min/max operation amount bounds exceeded and so on \
`NOT_SUFFICIENT_FUNDS` — operation is not allowed as source account/deposit has not enough funds \
`PARTY_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested party (beneficiary for example) is not available for operation (blocked for example)\
`OPERATION_NOT_APPLICABLE` — requested operation is not applicable for selected party

**Other errors** \
`NOT_FOUND` — requested resource/endpoint is not found \
`OTHER_CLIENT_ERROR` — any other client error \
`OTHER_SERVER_ERROR` — any other server error

details.description: Optional error details in JSON form
